data.title: Viasat to receive $568M under Ligado agreement
data.description:
• Viasat (NASDAQ:VSAT) expects to receive $568M from Ligado Networks in fiscal year 2026 under a term sheet that settles its subsidiary Inmarsat Global’s opposition to Ligado’s planned restructuring.
  • Starting on September 30, 2025, Ligado will start making quarterly payments of ~$16M per quarter to Inmarsat. The quarterly payment amount increases 3% per year for the life of the contract (through 2107).
  • Ligado will make two lump sum payments to Inmarsat: $420M on October 31, 2025, followed by $100M on March 31, 2026. Viasat will use the funds primarily to manage near term maturities and address its extended maturity profile.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Schwab total client assets rise 5% M/M to $10.35T in May
data.description:
• Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) on Friday said its total client assets rose 12% Y/Y and 5%M/M to $10.35 trillion as of month-end May.
  • Core net new assets brought to the company by new and existing clients increased 13% Y/Y to $35.0 billion in May.
  • New brokerage accounts opened during the month totaled 336,000, up 7% versus May 2024.
  • May trading volumes exceeding 7 million daily average trades for the 5th consecutive month, said the company.
  • Transactional sweep cash declined by $5.2 billion to end May at $398.1 billion.
  • The company said, "inclusive of clients’ engagement with markets and April tax seasonality, quarter-to-date transactional sweep cash is down less than 3% from March 2025 levels, helping us further reduce high-cost funding at the banks to approximately $32 billion as of May month-end."
  • SCHW -1.97% premarket to $86.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial announces $600M loan to aircraft lessor Aircastle
data.description:
Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (OTCPK:MBFJF) announced on Friday the close of a $600M unsecured term loan provided to the aircraft lessor, Aircastle.

The loan is expected to help Aircastle's working capital needs for general corporate purposes, including aircraft acquisitions, through April 2030.

Shares were -0.99% pre-market to $13.45.

data.title: SharpLink Gaming acquires ETH worth $463M; shares down 68%
data.description:
• SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET) announced the strategic acquisition of 176,270.69 ETH for an aggregate purchase price of $462.95M, at an average acquisition price of $2,626 per ETH.
  • This acquisition positions SBET as the largest publicly-traded holder of ETH in the world, and the second-largest holder behind the Ethereum Foundation.
  • SBET also disclosed it raised an additional $79M in approximate gross proceeds through its at-the-market facility.
  • With a majority of the ATM proceeds used to fund further purchases of ETH, contributing to the total above, these collective purchases have generated ETH per share growth of 11.8% since June 2, 2025.
  • As of June 13, 2025, over 95% of SharpLink’s ETH holdings are actively deployed in staking and liquid staking solutions.
  • SBET shares slump 68% premarket on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Unusual Machines to acquire Rotor Lab in $7M all-stock deal
data.description:
• Unusual Machines (NYSE:UMAC) on Friday said it has signed an agreement to acquire Rotor Lab Pty Ltd, an Australian developer and manufacturer of electric motors and propulsion systems for unmanned aerial systems (UAS).
  • The proposed acquisition is substantially an all-stock deal valued at $7 million (which includes a $3 million earnout).
  • Unusual Machines and Rotor Lab have already been working together for nearly a year, co-developing a family of motors including the 2207, 2807, and 3220 sizes.
  • The company also announced that it has notified Aloft that the previously announced acquisition has been terminated, effective June 9, 2025.
  • Unusual Machines said its business outlook remains unchanged, with no anticipated impact to operations, cash position, or strategic initiatives because of the canceled transaction.
  • UMAC +7.28% premarket to $8.84.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tony G Co-Investment announces name change to "HYLQ Strategy"
data.description:
• Tony G Co-Investment (BDRDF) intends to file articles of amendment to change its corporate name to "HYLQ Strategy Corp.".
  • The name change is expected to take effect on or around June 19, 2025.
  • The name change is to reflect the company's investments in the HyperLiquid ecosystem.
  • The company's trading symbol on the Canadian Securities Exchange is expected to change to "HYLQ". 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Stock index futures slide as Israel attacks Iran
data.description:
Stock index futures sank on Friday after Israel targeted Iran with a series of strikes against nuclear sites and military targets, raising fears of an all-out war in the Middle East. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.94%.

Iran also launched over 100 drones at Israel overnight in retaliation to Israel's "pre-emptive" strikes that targeted Iranian nuclear facilities and military sites late Thursday.

Israeli airspace has been closed, and a state of emergency has been declared as a counterattack was anticipated.

"The effects of the attack have cascaded across global markets, with a strong risk-off move for several asset classes," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Looking forward, the focus is now shifting to what form Iran’s retaliation might take. It’s also unclear whether talks between the U.S. and Iran over their nuclear program will continue, although AFP reported that the U.S. still wanted to hold talks this Sunday, Allen added.

"The scale of Israel’s air strikes against Iran was not anticipated by financial markets. As a result, the oil price has had the largest spike since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The economic disruption may well be contained—the starting point was quite a weak oil market, given expectations of a U.S. growth slowdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Wall Street on Thursday took home some gains, as investors weighed a positive producer inflation report and a strong Treasury bond auction against geopolitical risks and trade tensions.

The economic calendar is on the lighter side, with mainly the June Michigan Consumer Expectations report slated to land. Economists on average expect it to rise to 49.

Oil price moves do affect consumers’ inflation expectations, which tend to be dominated by food and fuel. Today’s Michigan inflation expectations data is no exception. It only matters economically if consumers change their behavior in response to shifts in expectations, which is rarely the case, Donovan said. 

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and other major cryptocurrencies also fell in early Friday trading after Israel launched airstrikes on Iran.

data.title: HA Sustainable Infrastructure prices $1B green senior unsecured notes offering
data.description:
HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (NYSE:HASI) announced on Friday the pricing of a registered public offering of $1B worth of green senior unsecured notes.

The investor in sustainable infrastructure assets advancing the energy transition said the offering is divided into two tranches - $600M of 6.15% green senior unsecured notes due 2031 and $400M of 6.75% green senior unsecured notes due 2034.

Shares were +0.84% pre-market to $27.48.

The settlement is expected to occur on June 24.

The net proceeds will be ~$987.3M, and are intended to be used to fund previously-announced cash tender offers or repay a portion of the outstanding borrowings under the unsecured revolving credit facility or commercial paper program.

"We will use cash equal to the net proceeds from this offering to acquire, invest in or refinance, in whole or in part, new and/or existing eligible green projects," according to a June 13 statement.

data.title: AmeraMex International secures $1.1M in equipment orders
data.description:
• AmeraMex International (OTCPK:AMMX) on Friday announced that it has secured $1.1 million in equipment orders and finalized a five-year rental agreement valued at $4.4 million.
  • The five-year rental agreement with the Army Base is for four Taylor Equipment Forklifts and worth $4.4 million over the life of the agreement.
  • Additionally, the company entered into a six-month rental agreement for two Taylor Equipment forklifts that have shipped to a customer in Idaho. The agreement will bring in $264,000 over the life of the agreement.
  • Alongside the rental deals, AmeraMex has facilitated multiple electric equipment sales, including two FIRSTGREEN Elise 900 electric skid steer loaders purchased by customers in California and LiuGong 865HE electric wheel loader delivered to a customer in California, among others.
  • Source: Press Release
